kennelTipLean.note: Two bottom-eight sides flopping into each other at Accor. Nobody's confident, half the thread reckons we'll cough it up off our own error, the other half reckon if we can't beat Volkman and Joash we should disband the club.

kennelTipQuotes.quote: If we can't beat a team with Volkman and Joash in the halves then we should disband — SARGE reckons we'll play good for 4 mins, make an error, then it's Eels 26 Dogs 10.

debates.question: Who runs the show in the halves on Monday?
debates.kennel: The Galvin v Critta thread is at 244 replies and the gameday thread's already arguing SOS over Galvin at 7.

options.label: Burton shifts, O'Sullivan gets a halves run
Burton 6, Galvin 7 but Galvin gets hooked late

debates.question: Who opens the scoring for the Dogs?
debates.kennel: Gameday thread's split between Kiraz being our only attacking weapon and a Burton special off a set play.

trivia.question: Which Bulldogs prop captained the club to the 2004 NRL Premiership?
trivia.explainer: Andrew 'Bobcat' Ryan captained the Dogs to the 2004 GF win over the Roosters — he was a back-rower/lock, not a prop. Trick question intent aside, Ryan is the answer.

recap.question: Who topped run metres for the Dogs in the Round 13 loss to the Tigers?
recap.explainer: Kiraz racked up 287 run metres — comfortably the most for the Dogs and more than any Tiger including Skelton (227).

washup.headline: Loss number eight and the 'going to click soon' line is now a meme.
washup.vibe: 16-22 to the Tigers, who beat us with Bula and Skelton having the time of their lives. Kiraz was our best on ground with 287m and a try, Galvin grabbed one late, but we were 18-0 down before we even showed up. Eight losses, 14th on the ladder, and now we're heading into a Monday afternoon clash with the 15th-placed Eels that nobody actually wants to watch.
washup.kennelSummary: Ciro's job thread cracked 578 replies inside 48 hours and the 'Season over' thread is at 216. The 'going to click soon' Foxsports line has become the running joke of the week — fans are using it ironically on every other post. Galvin v Critta has split the fanbase in half and the recruitment thread is now a coping mechanism for 2027.

kennelHotTakes.quote: Burton's the biggest flop to ever flop a 6 jersey — one good game every 25 and we all ruin our knee pads over it. Ciraldo's a white ant.
kennelHotTakes.author: JayBee

kennelThreadsThisWeek.title: Round 14 Canterbury Bankstown Parodies vs Scummy Eels - Monday 8th June 4:05pm - Accord Stadium, Sydney
kennelThreadsThisWeek.summary: Gameday thread's already at 309 replies and the mood is grim — half the room wants SOS at 7 and Burton at lock, the other half wants Galvin shipped to Flegg. PerthDog's line that we should disband if we lose to Volkman is the energy.

kennelThreadsLastGame.title: CIRALDOS JOB FUTURE
kennelThreadsLastGame.summary: 578 replies and counting after loss number 8. Johnnny2233's defending Ciro citing the two finals series, others reckon Gus's short fuse means a coach change is inevitable. Dingo's got the assistants in the crosshairs now too.

10.question: Salmon's named at 13 — does he start the game at lock?

options.label: No, Burton shifts to lock and Salmon goes bench
No, late change — Salmon out of the 17

10.question: Which edge do the Dogs target most in the first 20 minutes?

options.label: Through the middle — Galvin/Burton kicking game

11.question: Tracey's named at 1 — does he last the full 80 or get yanked early?

options.label: Shifted off fullback mid-game (Crichton or Burton goes back)

11.question: Where does the Dogs' first try come from?

options.label: Burton or Galvin set play / kick
Forward crash off a Hayward dart
Outside back off a broken-field scramble

12.question: Salmon's named at 13 — does he hold the lock spot for the full 80?
12.kennel: The 'why we've regressed' thread has half the Kennel screaming that Salmon at 13 is killing our attack.

options.label: Gets hooked early, Mann finishes at 13
Shifts into the middle, Preston rolls back to lock

12.question: First middle off the bench — who gets the early call against Utoikamanu and King?
12.kennel: Forum's been screaming 'shit middles' all week — bench rotation is going to matter the second the Storm pack rolls forward.

options.label: Jack Underhill gets the early call

13.question: Burton's named at 6 after his Storm masterclass — does he see out the full 80 there, or does Ciro shift him to lock late?
13.kennel: Magic Burton thread is unanimous he's our best 5/8 and only option there

options.label: Shifts to lock in the second half
Gets benched late with the game won

13.question: Who opens the scoring for the Dogs?
13.kennel: Rinakama thread is fizzing after his double v Storm, but Burton's the form player
